_meta.purpose: Deterministic, KEYLESS sector classification for the two SECTOR-SHAPED instruments (Chips Act 2.0 -> 'Semiconductors', CADA -> 'Cloud & AI'). No model calls. A candidate is scored per domain from (a) its sector/industry name and (b) a keyword pass over its business summary. Used by scripts/classify_sector.py.
_meta.inputs: sector + industry names and longBusinessSummary come from yfinance asset_profile (Yahoo taxonomy, GICS-aligned). When the FMP SOURCE is enabled later, FMP GICS sector/industry strings map onto the same fields; numeric GICS/ICB codes can be added under optional 'gics_codes' without code changes.
_meta.matching: Case-insensitive, word-boundary (\b<term>\w*) regex over the lowercased summary. Multi-word terms match as phrases. Each distinct term counts at most once.

scoring.formula: score = 2*(industry name in industry_match) + 1*(sector name in sector_match) + 1*(# distinct keyword hits); if ANY exclude_keyword hits, score := 0 (domain vetoed).
scoring.promote_requires_keyword_note: If true, a candidate with NO business-summary keyword hit (i.e. matched only by sector/industry code) caps at 'candidate' and is never promoted — stops broad Yahoo buckets like 'Software - Infrastructure' (payments/security/SaaS, e.g. Adyen) from auto-promoting on code alone.
scoring.tie_break: assign to the highest-scoring sector domain; equal scores -> the one with the industry_match hit, else neither (left for theme/none).
scoring.confidence_formula: round(min(0.95, 0.45 + 0.12*score), 2)  -- a transparent function of matched signals, NOT a model probability
scoring.note: All weights/thresholds here are tunable; sync_universe.py reads promote_score/candidate_score to set the bands. THRESHOLD env var can override promote_score at runtime.
